title: Continuity vs. Uniform Continuity in Metric Spaces
description: A flowchart illustrating the conceptual relationship between continuous and uniformly continuous functions on metric spaces, highlighting the key difference in how the delta is chosen.
elements: Uniformly Continuous Function (on the entire set X)
relationships: For a Continuous Function, δ can depend on both ε and the specific point p
For a Uniformly Continuous Function, δ depends only on ε and must work for every point p in X
Both functions map elements from (X, d_X) to (Y, d_Y) such that if d_X(x, p) < δ then d_Y(f(x), f(p)) < ε
